framework.name: Strong Customer Authentication (SCA) Exemption Model for Agents
framework.description: EU's PSD2 requires two-factor authentication for many online payments, which conflicts with autonomous agent operations. Solution requires either regulatory extensions/exemptions for pre-authorized autonomous payments or treating agent transactions similarly to existing exemptions for recurring payments or trusted beneficiaries.
framework.raw_quote: Similarly, in the EU's PSD2 regulations, Strong Customer Authentication requires two-factor auth for many online payments. If an AI agent is initiating a payment, it might not be feasible to involve the human for 2FA each time – regulators may need to create extensions or exemptions for pre-authorized autonomous payments (similar to how they treat recurring payments or trusted beneficiaries today).

source.title: Enabling Autonomous AI Agents to Make Payments - LinkedIn
